vitals.field_size: 3,000+ riders typically (2024: 3,100); 2026 cap not yet published
vitals.start_format: Staggered-start team time trial, teams released in waves
vitals.entry_fee: Not yet published for 2026 (2023: R13,250 per PPA-member team / R15,900 general / R17,800 corporate — historical pricing only)

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 70. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 66.

course_description.character: A circular 202 km team time trial looping out of Swellendam into the Overberg and Klein Karoo: the N2 to Suurbraak, up the R324 through Tradouw Pass, along the R62 through Op de Tradouw past Barrydale, through Montagu, over Cogmanskloof Pass into Ashton, out the R60 toward Robertson and Bonnievale, back through Rooibrug and Drew, and home to Swellendam via three closing climbs the organizer officially names the 'Three Stooges' (also called the 'Three Witches' by some riders, including in a first-person race recap). The organizer's published figure is roughly 2,100 m of climbing; the embedded Strava route reports 204.5 km and 2,192 m. Two real mountain passes come early-to-mid course, and the hardest climbing is saved for the final kilometers, so there's no coasting into the finish.
course_description.signature_challenge: The Three Stooges — the closing climbing trio inside the final kilometers, arriving after Tradouw Pass and Cogmanskloof Pass have already emptied the legs and after hours of team-paced racing have used up whatever margin a rider had left.
suffering_zones: Tradouw Pass (R324) — the first real mountain pass, early enough in the 202 km to set the tone for the day
Cogmanskloof Pass, between Montagu and Ashton — a second significant climb roughly mid-route
The Three Stooges — the organizer's name for the closing three climbs into Swellendam; some riders call them the 'Three Witches'

Team-pacing math — this is a staggered-start team time trial where the 6th rider across the line sets the official team time, so the team needs at least six riders through quickly, not just its strongest individuals

logistics.lodging_strategy: Unknown; Western Cape region offers various lodging options in nearby towns and wine country areas

history.origin_story: The inaugural 'Double Century' (DC) ran in 1993 with 385 starters at Natte Valleij near Klapmuts, formalizing informal 200 km club rides that Charles Milner and other local cyclists had been organizing in the early 1990s. The event moved through Drakenstein Correctional Facility, then Ceres and Montagu, before settling in Swellendam, its current home. Across that lineage it has carried a series of sponsorship eras: Pedal Power/PPA Double Century (early 2000s), Coronation Double Century (2007-2019, under investment manager Coronation Fund Managers), a sponsor-free Pedal Power Association Double Century in the COVID-adjusted 2020 edition, and Old Mutual Wealth Double Century from 2021 onward.

biased_opinion.verdict: Established Team Time Trial, 2026 Price Pending
biased_opinion.summary: The Old Mutual Wealth Double Century is a team time trial with roots to 1993, though the event itself has moved venues repeatedly (Natte Valleij/Klapmuts, Drakenstein, Ceres, Montagu) before settling in Swellendam, and has run through several sponsorship eras since (Pedal Power/PPA, Coronation Fund Managers, a sponsor-free 2020 edition, and Old Mutual Wealth since 2021) plus a full weather cancellation in 2021. The current course covers Tradouw Pass, Cogmanskloof Pass, and a closing climbing trio the organizer calls the 'Three Stooges' for roughly 2,100 m over 202 km. It's a team format first — the official time is set by the 6th rider across the line, so the team needs at least six riders through quickly rather than relying on a couple of strong individuals — and as of this research the organizer hasn't published 2026 pricing, four months out from a late-November event that historically draws 3,000+ riders.
strengths: A continuous lineage back to 1993, one of South Africa's longest-running mass team road events, despite venue changes and a 2021 weather cancellation
Real mountain-pass climbing at roughly 2,100 m — Tradouw Pass and Cogmanskloof Pass mid-route, then the Three Stooges saved for the finish — not a flat social ride
Large, established field (3,000+ typically, 3,100 riders in 2024) with a dedicated Charles Milner Medal for teams whose all 12 riders finish together, within 10 minutes of the team's first finisher
Flexible team size (6-12 riders) lowers the bar to field a squad without needing a full 12-person roster
weaknesses: As of this research, the 2026 entry fee is not yet published — the official entry page still says 'coming soon' for an event roughly four months out; no 2026-specific field cap has been listed either, though the general event page describes a roughly 250-team event
It's a team time trial, not an individual race: the 6th rider across the line sets the official time, meaning the team must get at least six riders through together, not just its fastest individuals
Real weather risk — the 2021 edition was cancelled on race morning due to flooding and rockfalls on the route, not a hypothetical
biased_opinion.bottom_line: A legitimate, long-running team road test with real climbing — worth entering if you can field a committed 6-12 rider squad. Don't book travel around firm pricing yet: as of this research the 2026 entry fee isn't published.

final_verdict.one_liner: A Cape Winelands team grind dating to 1993 — Tradouw Pass, Cogmanskloof, and the Three Stooges close it out.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes, if you can put together a committed 6-12 rider team and want a genuine mountain-pass road test with three decades of history behind it. Hold off finalizing travel plans until the 2026 entry fee is published — as of this research, pricing for 2026 hasn't been announced. Note the course data itself has some slack: the organizer markets 202 km/2,100 m, while the embedded Strava route reports 204.5 km/2,192 m.
final_verdict.alternatives: For a similar South African mass-participation road event without the team-time-trial format, look at the Cape Town Cycle Tour (individual, flatter, Cape Peninsula) or the 947 Ride Joburg (individual, urban, less climbing) — neither replicates the Double Century's team structure or its mountain-pass course.

videos.title: The 2023 Old Mutual Wealth Double Century
videos.description:
The Old Mutual Wealth Double Century is around the corner - Saturday, 25 November 2023 will be the 31st edition in Swellendam. This is where friendships are cemented and memories are made that last far longer than the 202km you will spend together on the roads of the Western Cape. Entries for this bucket list event open on Monday, 14 August.

For more information, please visit: https://doublecentury.co.za/

videos.title: Old Mutual Double Century 2024 Highlights
videos.description: Relive the highlights of the 2024 Old Mutual Wealth Double Century!  From breathtaking views to unmatched teamwork and grit, the Old Mutual Wealth Double Century Highlights package is a must-watch! Catch the highlights and soak in the unforgettable moments from the race!

videos.title: Old Mutual Double Century 2023
videos.description:
Old Mutual DC 2023, with Team DVCC - Jumbo Vino

D’Ville Cycles, also known as DVCC entered the Old Mutual Wealth Double Century that happened on the 25th of November 2023.  This gruelling race takes place over 202km, starts in Swellendam and visits Suurbraak and Tradouw Pass, Op de Tradouw, Montague and Ashton while side-swiping Barrydale, Robertson and Bonnievale before heading back to Swellendam where the Three Stooges, also known as the Three Witches awaits.  What nobody tells you is the two sisters of the Three Stooges.  The overall elevation is approximately 2100 meters of climbing, a race definitely not for the faint-hearted.

DVCC entered three teams, the start order of the teams is the slower teams go first with a 30-second gap between teams.  The first team to start from mDVCC is called "Di12" (start time was 06:03:00).  Followed by ourselves, "Jumbo Vino" (06:20:00), and then lastly the racing team "Kirmack" (07:10:00).

The objectives of the racing teams are different from the social teams.  The racing team only need to get 6 riders over the finish line.  Whereas our objective was to get all 12 riders over the line at the same time (or within a minute of each other).  Succeeding in that objective grants you the Charles Milner medal as well.

Some interesting parts to watch out for in the video:
3:31, ~65km, we passed Di12 and Kirmak made up close to 33 minutes on us.  Most of the climbing is now done (our weakness) so now it is time to slip behind some teams and recover from the climbs.

4:23, ~85km, we have used up most of the slips of the teams.  Time to put the hammer down.

5:02, 115km, the first neutral zone

5:36, 118km, Kirmack exited the feeding zone 4 minutes behind us.

6:04, 135km, Kirmarck passed us.

7:08, 165km, second neutral zone.  Our one rider missed the feeding truck and had to turn around.

8:11, 182km, the first witch

8:18, 186km, the second witch

8:38 190km, the third witch

9:00, riders waiting at the finish line.

9:03, falling back to help a teammate finish the last climb.


Jumbo Vino's official time is 06:52; our unofficial time was closer to 06:35 as we waited at the finish line for our one teammate who had to walk/run up the last hill due to cramps.

More information about the event: https://doublecentury.co.za/
videos.curation_reason: First-person team race recap with specific course details including Tradouw Pass, Montague, Ashton and the famous 'Three Witches' climbs.

race_day_tips.text: The event emphasizes teamwork and camaraderie - doing it as a team makes the tough journey much easier.

additional_quotes.text: It's the best end of year party for all cyclists in the country.
This is the event of the year something that gets friends together has a great time in the countryside of the Western Cape.

rider_intel.search_text: The Old Mutual Wealth Double Century in Swellendam, Western Cape is described by riders as a team-oriented event emphasizing camaraderie and teamwork. Participants highlight that while it's a tough journey, doing it as a team makes the experience much easier. The race is characterized as the best end-of-year party for cyclists in South Africa, focusing on bringing friends together for a great day out in the Western Cape countryside. The event is noted for its unique team format and strong community atmosphere, with riders emphasizing the social and collaborative aspects over individual competition. The race takes place in the scenic countryside around Swellendam.

eligibility.notes: 2026 edition confirmed for Saturday, November 21, 2026, in Swellendam per official homepage and Pedal Power Association calendar. Continuous event since 1993 under five sponsor-era names (Double Century -> Pedal Power/PPA Double Century -> Coronation Double Century 2007-2019 -> sponsor-free 2020 -> Old Mutual Wealth Double Century 2021-present); Old Mutual Wealth renewed sponsorship per PPA's September 2025 CEO report, so 'Old Mutual Wealth Double Century' remains the correct current name, not a stale sponsor tag. The 2021 edition was cancelled race-morning for weather (flooding/rockfalls), not a series break. 2026 entry fee not yet published as of this research.
